title: Place a Return Tote on the Hospital Station Induct Side
summary: Return a tote to the system from the hospital station by placing the tote on the left-side induct area of the station and ensuring it is flush against the end stops.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when a tote at the hospital station is going back into the system and must be physically placed on the station induct side.
not_for: Do not use this procedure for totes that are not going back into the system.
Do not use this procedure as a complete hospital station workflow; the source only supports the physical tote placement portion.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Place the tote on the hospital station induct side and ensure the tote is flush against the end stops.

safety_notes: Use only the documented physical placement steps supported by the source.
The source does not provide additional handling instructions if the tote cannot be positioned flush against the end stops.
access_or_tools_needed: Physical access to the hospital station induct side
Tote to be returned to the system

access_required: Physical access to the hospital station
system_preconditions: The tote is at the hospital station.
The tote is going back into the system.
external_references: OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual, section 5.9 Hospital Station Operation

visual_references.description: Hospital station tote handling image from page 92 showing the hospital station context for tote return handling.
Hospital HMI Add Tote screen showing the add-tote context for sending a tote back into the system after it is positioned properly on the induct side.

steps.title: Identify the hospital station induct side
steps.purpose: Ensure the tote is placed on the correct side of the hospital station for return into the system.
steps.instruction: Identify the induct side of the hospital station. The source identifies the induct side as the left side of the station.
steps.expected_result: The operator has identified the left-side induct area of the hospital station.
steps.healthy_condition: The left side of the station is clearly identified as the induct side.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot determine the induct side from the available source-supported station view or local markings.

actions.label: Locate induct side
actions.exact_action: Identify the induct side (left side) of the station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital station view showing the tote handling area and the left-side induct location.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the induct side cannot be confidently identified from the source-supported station context.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If a tote is going back into the system, put the tote on the induct side (left side) of the station.
Artifact appears on page 92 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_5_9_hospital_station_operation.

steps.title: Place the tote on the induct side
steps.purpose: Position the tote in the correct return location so it can go back into the system.
steps.instruction: If the tote is going back into the system, place the tote on the induct side of the hospital station.
steps.expected_result: The tote is placed on the left-side induct area of the station.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote is physically resting on the induct side of the station.
steps.failure_condition: The tote is not placed on the induct side or cannot be placed there.

actions.label: Place tote on induct side
actions.exact_action: Put the tote on the induct side (left side) of the station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital station tote handling area associated with placing a tote back into the system.
Add Tote screen context indicating the tote is sent back into the system after it is positioned properly on the induct side.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the tote is not intended to go back into the system.
Escalate if the tote cannot be placed on the induct side and the source provides no alternate handling steps.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If a tote is going back into the system, put the tote on the induct side (left side) of the station.
The operator uses the green scanner to scan the tote barcode, which populates the Tote ID and Barcode fields, then presses Confirm & Execute after the tote is positioned properly on the induct side.

steps.title: Align the tote against the end stops
steps.purpose: Ensure the tote is in the documented final position for return into the system.
steps.instruction: Position the tote so it is flush against the end stops.
steps.expected_result: The tote is flush against the end stops on the induct side.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote is fully seated against the end stops with no visible gap.
steps.failure_condition: The tote cannot be positioned flush against the end stops or remains misaligned.

actions.label: Seat tote against end stops
actions.exact_action: Ensure the tote is flush against the end stops.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: End-stop area and tote placement position used to verify the tote is flush against the stops.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the tote cannot be positioned flush against the end stops.
Stop if the final tote alignment cannot be verified.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Ensure the tote is flush against the end stops.
Artifact appears on page 92 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_5_9_hospital_station_operation.

success_criteria: The tote is placed on the hospital station induct side.
The induct side used is the left side of the station.
The tote is flush against the end stops.
healthy_conditions: The operator can identify the left-side induct area.
The tote is positioned on the induct side for return into the system.
The tote is seated flush against the end stops.
failure_conditions: The tote is placed on the wrong side of the station.
The tote is not flush against the end stops.
The source does not provide additional recovery steps if correct flush positioning cannot be achieved.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the tote cannot be positioned flush against the end stops.
Escalate if the operator cannot determine the correct induct-side placement from the available source-supported station context.
Use SME review because the source does not provide additional handling or recovery instructions for misalignment.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital station tote handling image associated with left-side induct placement and end-stop alignment.
Hospital HMI Add Tote screen showing the tote return context after proper induct-side positioning.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: If a tote is going back into the system, put the tote on the induct side (left side) of the station.
Ensure the tote is flush against the end stops.
Artifact appears on page 92 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_5_9_hospital_station_operation.

missing_details: The source does not provide a time estimate for this procedure.
The source does not provide additional recovery or alternate handling steps if the tote cannot be positioned flush against the end stops.
The source does not specify whether production stop or LOTO is required.
The source does not provide a direct confirmation indicator that the tote has been accepted back into the system within this narrow physical-placement procedure.
